sentenceStructure.openingStyle: Bold statement opener - single punchy sentence under 10 words
sentenceStructure.paragraphPattern: Short paragraphs, never more than 2-3 sentences. Short-Long-Short rhythm alternating punchy statements with longer explanatory sentences.

avoidList: at the end of the day

rhetoric.hookPattern: Bold statement opener - 2-8 words, period, sometimes with emoji. Never preamble.
rhetoric.ctaStyle: Community invitation / compare notes / invitation to dialogue

canonicalExamples.why: Bold statement opener with signature emoji trio - sets energetic tone in 2 words

canonicalExamples.text: My Dad disarmed live bombs for work.
canonicalExamples.why: Dramatic personal hook - 7 words that compel reading. Highest-engagement post opener.

canonicalExamples.text: 🚀 AI is changing marketing, whether you're ready or not.
canonicalExamples.why: Certainty-led strategic opener with rocket emoji energy

canonicalExamples.text:
My Dad disarmed live bombs for work. Circa 1970s SFPD, before robots and padded armor, he wore a suit & tie while kneeling in streets.

He and his partner, with police colleagues globally, founded the International Association of Bomb Technicians and Investigators. To my family, friends, and countless kids-now-adults in Scouts, Camp Fire Girls, and the St Peter’s Teen Club in Pacifica, my Dad’s known as a badass.

He’s now 86. Health is precarious. He recovered enough a few days ago to return home from a two-week hospital stay, the second in last 18 mos. My mom broke her hip in April. My sister, brother, and I are sandwiched between supporting our parents while parenting our own kids. And in my case, solo parenting.

In three weeks, I’m facilitating an exec roundtable on sandwich and single parenting while leading amid high visibility, pressure, and always-on stakes.

If you can relate, pls share your wisdom, rants, and questions. I know one truism for sure: it takes a village. 💃 👩‍👧‍👦 ❤️
canonicalExamples.why: Highest engagement post - personal vulnerability with professional pivot, signature vocabulary ('badass,' 'pls,' 'village'), emoji signature close

canonicalExamples.text:
Positioning. It’s hard, essential, and game changing.

I’ve led positioning for small, large, public, private, and nonprofit organizations and I still love the fundamentals.

Thank you guru Bob Wright for bringing the truisms and Matt Heinz for convening power players this morning. Loved seeing you Claire Darling, Paramita Bhattacharya, Barbara Leavitt, and Shelli Strand. And here’s to more convo, Justin Pirie and Jeff Yoshimura. 🚀
canonicalExamples.why: Credentials + community warmth pattern. Bold opener, experience credibility, generous tagging, rocket emoji close.

canonicalExamples.text: If you can relate, pls share your wisdom, rants, and questions. I know one truism for sure: it takes a village. 💃 👩‍👧‍👦 ❤️
canonicalExamples.why: Community-oriented close with casual abbreviation ('pls'), truism framing, and signature emoji sequence

canonicalExamples.text: If you're thinking through how AI changes your marketing org, your roadmap, or your role as a CMO, I'd love to compare notes.
canonicalExamples.why: Peer-level invitation to dialogue - positions her as equal, not authority

canonicalExamples.text: Pre-draft research: help me, hive, how do I win?!
canonicalExamples.why: Playful community ask using signature 'hive' address

openingPatterns.pattern: 2-8 words. Period. Sometimes with emoji. No preamble.
examples: Positioning. It's hard, essential, and game changing.
🚀 AI is changing marketing, whether you're ready or not.

openingPatterns.pattern: Dramatic personal statement. Storytelling lead. Personal before professional.
examples: My Dad disarmed live bombs for work.

openingPatterns.pattern: Poses a question then immediately answers or reframes it.
examples: The question isn't "Should we use AI?" That debate is over.

openingPatterns.pattern: Direct address to network using 'hive' or playful framing.
examples: Hey hive -- I'm looking for recommendations...
In a bold Any-Given-Friday leap into the unknown...

placementRules: Opening: Single emoji or signature trio to set tone
Closing: Heart emoji with gratitude posts, dancing emoji with personal posts
Lists: Light bulb for insights, check marks for actions, arrows for sequences
Never: More than 3 emojis in a row except signature opener
Topic-specific emojis match context (football for fantasy posts, microphone for podcast)

structuralPatterns.name: Personal Story > Professional Insight > Call to Engage
structure: Extended narrative with emotional detail and chronological flow
Pivot to professional relevance (exec roundtable, career parallel)
Call for community input with 'pls' and signature emoji

structuralPatterns.name: Certainty Lead > Bullet Framework > Peer Invitation
structure: Reframe the question: 'The question isn't X. It's Y.'
Peer invitation close: 'I'd love to compare notes'

structuralPatterns.name: Energy Burst > Ask
structuralPatterns.useCase: Short commentary, requests, playful community posts

neverUse: at the end of the day
formattingToAvoid: Excessive hashtags - max 6, only at end of strategic posts
Over-formatted posts - minimal bold, no headers in posts
Rhetorical questions in openers - she states, doesn't ask
All-caps for emphasis - uses emojis instead
toneToAvoid: Overly promotional self-talk without peer-gratitude frame
Skipping the human element in business posts
Closing without invitation or emoji warmth

hashtagStyle.placement: Always at the end, never inline. Only on strategic/thought leadership posts.

taggingStyle.frequency: EXTREMELY generous with @mentions in community/event posts. Minimal to none in personal vulnerability posts.
patterns: Full names (first + last) for LinkedIn tagging functionality
Often with relationship context: 'Thank you Karina for inviting me'
Grouped by context: 'Kudos and thank you 6sense!'
Flowing text format: names embedded in narrative, not standalone lists
specialPhrases: Special thanks for great convos and inspiration from badass leaders [names]
And here's to more convo, [Name] and [Name]
